V-M-058.checks: Frontend-admin exposes compact MTProto analytics, alert review, and promotion tag controls without secret leakage, visible technical Signals noise, hidden existing controls, or mobile layout regressions.
V-M-058.commands: pass-local 2026-06-06: cd frontend-admin && npm run build; node scripts/phase43-mtproto-admin-analytics-smoke.mjs; node scripts/phase54-admin-matrix-ui-smoke.mjs; node scripts/phase79-cyber-title-and-mtproto-promo-safe-smoke.mjs
V-M-058.evidence: Frontend-admin build passed and Phase-42/Phase-43/Phase-54/Phase-79 static smokes confirm compact Overview/Users/Abuse/Settings UI, removed Recent events, hidden visible Signals panel/count, paginated proxy/user lists, compact area graphs, source-IP unavailable state, auto-refresh markers, Russian hover help for alert review actions, compact scrollable IP history without visible CIDR prefixes, open inbox/archive split, no-dot empty states, promo-sharing allowed copy, manual-only enforcement copy, and redaction guards.

verification-levels.module-local: Build frontend-admin and run static UI smoke for route wiring, API names, redaction strings, confirmation flows, and mobile viewport overflow.
verification-levels.wave-level: Run after V-M-057 to prove UI contracts match backend schemas for analytics and promotion tag state.
verification-levels.phase-level: Phase-42 cannot close unless admin can scan global and per-proxy analytics from mobile without exposing secrets or losing existing MTProto ops controls.
verification-levels.phase-43-level: Phase-43 cannot execute unless UI gates cover removed Recent events, Overview/Users/Abuse/Settings layout, long Top users, graphs, auto-refresh, raw-IP investigation scope, and mobile compactness.
verification-levels.phase-54-level: Phase-54 cannot close unless the Matrix admin redesign keeps MTProto analytics dense, compact, auto-refreshing, and readable without restoring visible technical Signals noise.
verification-levels.phase-79-level: Phase-79 cannot close unless visible MTProto alert copy frames sharing as expected promo usage and enforcement as manual admin action only.

phase-43-checks.check-1: Recent events section is absent from the visible admin MTProto analytics page.
phase-43-checks.check-2: Overview, Users, Abuse, and Settings surfaces fit mobile widths without nested cards or horizontal overflow.
phase-43-checks.check-3: Top users supports 25, 50, and 100 item views with scrollable compact rows.
phase-43-checks.check-4: User detail drawer shows issued_at, last_seen_at, current IP, last IP, all IPs for 90 days, sessions, traffic, duration, and errors.
phase-43-checks.check-5: Graphs render traffic, connections, active connections, duration, and errors without layout shift during auto-refresh.
phase-43-checks.check-6: CPU/RAM/runtime metric tiles auto-refresh and show stale/unavailable states clearly.
phase-43-checks.check-7: Raw IP strings render only inside the admin investigation drawer and are excluded from static smoke fixtures, summaries, and logs.
phase-43-checks.check-8: Assignment inventory and user investigation search expose bounded page sizes with next/previous controls for large proxy sets.
phase-43-checks.check-9: Abuse open inbox queries only open alerts and archive surfaces acknowledged/resolved alert history.
phase-43-checks.check-10: Abuse empty states are padded for readability and do not end with stray punctuation.
phase-43-checks.check-11: Visible technical Signals panel/count is absent from the admin MTProto UI while alert APIs remain wired.
phase-43-checks.check-12: Open alert action buttons expose short Russian hover descriptions for review, acknowledge, resolve, disable, and follow-up TTL IP block decisions.
phase-43-checks.check-13: IP history uses a bounded internal scroll area and renders only address/hash rows, not address/prefix mask rows.

phase-79-checks.check-1: Open Abuse/Alerts copy must make clear that normal proxy sharing for promotion is allowed and alerts are hard-abuse candidates for admin review.
phase-79-checks.check-2: The visible UI must not render technical Signals panels, signal counters, auto-block wording, auto-disable wording, or any implication that sharing is punished automatically.
phase-79-checks.check-3: Disable proxy and TTL IP block buttons remain explicit admin actions with confirmation and Russian hover help before the operator clicks.
phase-79-checks.check-4: Archive behavior remains intact: resolved/acknowledged/actioned alerts leave the open inbox but remain reviewable.
phase-79-checks.check-5: Redaction smoke must reject full proxy links, fake-TLS secrets, raw promotion tags, runtime tokens, and raw IP values outside the explicit investigation drawer.

phase-54-checks.check-1: Matrix /mtproto analytics keeps Overview, Users, Abuse, and Settings tabs compact and reachable on mobile.
phase-54-checks.check-2: Graphs for traffic, connections, active connections, duration, and errors render in stable bounded containers without layout shift during refresh.
phase-54-checks.check-3: Top users remains long-list friendly through page-size controls or bounded scroll, not unstructured document scrolling.
phase-54-checks.check-4: User investigation drawer keeps issued time, last seen, current IP, last IP, IP history, sessions, graph data, and errors readable with internal scrolling.
phase-54-checks.check-5: Abuse shows only Alerts plus Archive; visible technical Signals panel/count remains absent.
phase-54-checks.check-6: Promotion tag control shows masked current tag, validation state, pending_restart/applied/failed state, and confirmation copy without raw tag leakage.
phase-54-checks.check-7: Resource CPU/RAM/runtime metrics auto-refresh and show stale/unavailable states without resizing Matrix panels.
phase-54-checks.check-8: Raw IP values are limited to the explicit investigation drawer and remain excluded from smoke logs, general cards, and screenshots when fixtures can use masked data.

module-checks.check-1: Admin can scan global summary, traffic windows, top users, active connections, errors, runtime health, and availability proof on mobile.
module-checks.check-2: Per-proxy detail exposes last seen, session count, duration, traffic, errors, and alert context without hiding reissue/revoke controls.
module-checks.check-3: Filters for day, week, month, event type, status, and assignment do not cause horizontal overflow at 360px width.
module-checks.check-4: Technical abuse signals are not rendered as a separate visible admin panel; only high/critical Alerts plus Archive are shown.
module-checks.check-5: Promotion tag editing requires explicit confirmation and renders only masked current tag after save.
module-checks.check-6: Static smoke confirms no full tg links, fake-TLS secrets, runtime tokens, raw promotion tags, or raw IP values render in the UI bundle.

scenarios.scenario-1: Admin can scan global summary, traffic windows, top users, active connections, and error counts on mobile.
scenarios.scenario-2: Per-proxy detail is reachable without hiding reissue/revoke controls.
scenarios.scenario-3: Admin opens Abuse and sees only Alerts plus Archive, without the technical Signals list or signal counter.
scenarios.scenario-4: Static smoke confirms no full tg links, secrets, tokens, raw promotion tags, or raw IP values render in the UI.
scenarios.scenario-5: Promotion tag form handles valid, invalid, pending_restart, applied, and failed states.
scenarios.scenario-6: Phase-43 admin opens a high-abuse alert, reviews user/IP/session detail, and disables the selected proxy through confirmation UI.
scenarios.scenario-7: Auto-refresh updates summary, alerts, top users, graph data, and resource metrics without duplicating requests or resizing controls.
scenarios.scenario-8: Admin acknowledges, resolves, disables, or blocks from an alert; the alert leaves the open list and remains reviewable in the archive.
scenarios.scenario-9: Phase-54 admin scans MTProto analytics on a phone and can still drill into one user without losing the overview context.
scenarios.scenario-10: Phase-54 visual effects do not obscure CPU/RAM runtime metrics, availability proof, alert action buttons, or promotion tag status.
scenarios.scenario-11: Admin sees a high/critical alert and understands it is a manual-review candidate; no UI copy says the proxy or IP was automatically blocked.
scenarios.scenario-12: Admin investigates a heavily shared promo proxy and still sees usage/IP history without a visible technical Signals panel or automatic punishment state.

redaction-rules.rule-1: UI renders masked SNI and masked promotion tags only.
redaction-rules.rule-2: Full proxy links remain limited to user-owned cabinet flows, not admin analytics lists.
redaction-rules.rule-3: Raw IP values may appear only in explicit admin investigation drawer fixtures; runtime token, full tag, base secret, salt, private keys, and proxy secrets may never appear in generated bundles or page render fixtures.

autonomous-execution-safety.safe-local: Frontend build and static smoke are safe to run locally.
autonomous-execution-safety.blocked-live: Visual/live admin validation against production data requires operator approval and redacted screenshots only.

evidence.phase-54-verification-design-2026-06-03: Phase-54 verification design added Matrix analytics gates for compact tabs, stable graphs, top-users list, alert inbox/archive, IP history, resource metrics, promotion tag redaction, auto-refresh, and no visible Signals regression.
evidence.phase-54-local-2026-06-03: Frontend-admin build and Phase-54 smoke passed for compact Matrix analytics: CHARTS_TABLES_READABLE, AUTO_REFRESH_STABLE, SIGNALS_STILL_HIDDEN, PROMOTION_TAG_REDACTED, bounded user/top-user/alert/IP-history lists, and protected-surface isolation.
evidence.phase-79-local-2026-06-06: Frontend-admin build and Phase-79 smoke passed for promo-safe alert copy, hidden visible Signals, manual-only enforcement wording, promotion tag redaction, and no full proxy-link exposure.

phase-79-verification-design.commands: cd frontend-admin && npm run build; node scripts/phase43-mtproto-admin-analytics-smoke.mjs; node scripts/phase54-admin-matrix-ui-smoke.mjs; node scripts/phase79-cyber-title-and-mtproto-promo-safe-smoke.mjs
phase-79-verification-design.acceptance: MTProto admin Abuse UI stays compact and redacted, keeps Signals hidden, presents sharing as expected promotion behavior, and exposes only manual confirmed enforcement actions.
phase-79-verification-design.evidence: PASS local 2026-06-06: admin build and Phase-43/54/79 smokes passed.
